_meta.credit: Structure adapted from borski/travel-hacking-toolkit (MIT) — github.com/borski/travel-hacking-toolkit. Data independently re-verified for 2026.
_meta.definition: Stopover = planned interruption >24h (intl) or >4h (domestic). Some programs allow free stopovers, some charge points/cash, most US carriers no longer allow them at all.
_meta.notes: United Excursionist Perk DISCONTINUED 2025-08-21. Confirmed live: Aeroplan, Alaska Atmos (post-rebrand), Icelandair, TAP, Turkish, Cathay, Flying Blue, ANA, JAL, Korean, Singapore KrisFlyer, Emirates, Copa, Etihad, LH M&M.

allowed.rule: 1 stopover on one-way; 2 stopovers on round-trip (1 per direction)
allowed.notes: Best stopover deal alive in 2026. Combine with LH First or ANA First for two-destination trip at near-zero marginal cost.

allowed.rule: 1 free stopover on a one-way partner award (2 round-trip)
allowed.notes: Mileage Plan -> Atmos rebrand preserved this rule. Single-partner-per-ticket restriction still applies. Allow 30+ min on phone.

allowed.rule: 1 free stopover in Istanbul on award itineraries
allowed.notes: Famously hard to reach by phone; persistence required.

allowed.rule: Free stopover in Lisbon (LIS) or Porto (OPO), 1-10 nights
allowed.notes: Also available on TAP cash fares. Partner-discount perks included (hotels, experiences).

allowed.rule: Free stopover in Reykjavik (KEF) on every transatlantic itinerary
allowed.notes: 70+ year program. Saga Premium Flex up to 21 days. Bookable via JetBlue TrueBlue partner award (added 2024) — opens path from Bilt/Chase UR. Single best free-second-country perk in commercial aviation.

allowed.rule: Saver: 1 stopover RT for $100 (SGD). Advantage: 2 stopovers RT free.
Free stopover in Dubai (DXB) on certain partner award routings
allowed.notes: Less generous than other ME3 — Etihad and Qatar both more flexible.

allowed.rule: Free stopover in Panama City (PTY) on award itineraries
Up to 3 stopovers allowed on intl partner award (1 in JP, others vary)
allowed.geography: Partner awards only; JAL metal more restrictive
allowed.notes: Among the most generous OW stopover rules.

allowed.rule: 2 stopovers + 2 open jaws + 5 transfers on RT multi-carrier award
allowed.notes: OneWorld multi-carrier awards are where this shines.

allowed.rule: Effectively unlimited stopovers on phone-booked award itineraries
allowed.notes: Some agents wrongly claim only AMS/CDG hub stopovers — push back, cite FAQ.

allowed.rule: 1 free stopover on intl partner round-trip awards
allowed.notes: RT-only program — stopover essentially built into the structure.

allowed.rule: 1 free stopover on Star Alliance partner awards
allowed.notes: Devalued May 2026 — confirm rule still applies on multi-segment awards.

allowed.rule: 1 free stopover in Abu Dhabi (AUH) on award itineraries
1 free stopover in Doha (DOH) on certain awards
allowed.notes: Less generous than other ME3 stopovers; agent-dependent.

allowed.rule: Up to 2 stopovers + 2 open jaws on round-trip award
allowed.notes: Heavy YQ surcharges are the catch.

allowed.rule: 1 free stopover on round-trip intl awards

discontinued.rule_was: Free one-way award within multi-city itineraries of 3+ one-ways with specific geographic/cabin conditions
discontinued.notes: Eliminated August 21, 2025 alongside instant upgrades and upgrade chart.

not_allowed.notes: Long layovers up to 24h international permitted but no stopover perk
Long layovers up to 24h international permitted only
Each segment priced separately as own award
Revenue-based, segment-priced (but Icelandair partner award unlocks KEF stopover)
No formal stopover program on awards
Routes converting to Alaska Atmos through Apr 2026; old HA rules dead
Excursionist Perk discontinued Aug 2025; no stopover replacement
